nodes.label: AI as collaborative sounding board for ideas
nodes.label_negative: AI as autonomous creator replacing human thought
nodes.grounding_span: it's been a great sounding board for ideas

nodes.label: AI as efficiency tool for research and editing
nodes.grounding_span: it primarily has just become a bit faster and easier

nodes.label: Human-originated creative work
nodes.label_negative: AI-generated creative work claimed as human
nodes.grounding_span: creative works need to originate from the human brain

nodes.label: AI over-reliance in writing and education
nodes.label_negative: Balanced AI use preserving human thinking
nodes.grounding_span: people leaning too heavily on AI for writing and creative tasks

nodes.label: Creative authenticity and originality
nodes.grounding_span: I have a real thing about that

nodes.label: Productivity and ease in workflow
nodes.grounding_span: it primarily has just become a bit faster and easier

nodes.label: Human cognitive development and independent thinking
nodes.grounding_span: impact on people's ability to think for themselves

nodes.label: Gratitude for AI's organizational help
nodes.grounding_span: I was very grateful for this new tool

nodes.label: Concern about AI replacing human creativity in publishing
nodes.grounding_span: I am concerned that will lead to a lot of books that are very derivative

nodes.label: Concern about AI hindering student learning
nodes.grounding_span: I'm very concerned about the impact that will have on their learning to think

nodes.label: Commitment to human-driven creative process
nodes.grounding_span: I, myself, will probably continue to do the bulk of my writing the old fashioned way

nodes.label: Iterative back-and-forth ideation with AI
nodes.grounding_span: we can go back and forth with iterations of the ideas

nodes.label: Principled boundary-setting around AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I am fairly set on the idea that creative works need to originate from the human brain
